title: Thenumberofnewfastfoodrestaurantopeningsiscorrelatedwiththepriceofbitcoin
thesis: Expansion of fast food chains indicates growing consumer confidence and disposable income, which may drive increased investment in speculative assets like Bitcoin. Technology and cryptocurrency sectors are sensitive to consumer sentiment and retail investment trends.
signal_logic: If the 4-week moving average of new fast food openings increases by more than 10% compared to 3 months ago
entry_exit: Entry: If the 4-week moving average of new fast food openings increases by more than 10% compared to 3 months ago Exit: After 2 weeks or if the 4-week average stops increasing
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Fast Food Industry Reports via scrape (weekly).

schema.idea: The number of new fast food restaurant openings is correlated with the price of Bitcoin
schema.behavior: Expansion of fast food chains indicates growing consumer confidence and disposable income, which may drive increased investment in speculative assets like Bitcoin.

market_impact.rationale: Technology and cryptocurrency sectors are sensitive to consumer sentiment and retail investment trends.

trade_logic.entry: If the 4-week moving average of new fast food openings increases by more than 10% compared to 3 months ago
trade_logic.exit: After 2 weeks or if the 4-week average stops increasing
